statement.markdown:
Given a stack of integers, implement a method to count the number of elements in the stack.
You can assume that there are only positive integers in the stack. The input is a string of space-separated integers.
The method should return the correct count of elements in the stack.
Hint: Be careful with empty strings as input.
statement.inputFormat: A string of space-separated integers, possibly empty
statement.outputFormat: The correct count of elements in the stack

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int countElements(string s);}  // TODO: implement
starterCode.java: public class Solution {public int countElements(String s)} // TODO: implement
starterCode.python: class Solution: def countElements(self, s):  # TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: int countElements(string s) { istringstream iss(s); int count = 0; while (iss >> count) {} return count; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public int countElements(String s) { Scanner scanner = new Scanner(s); int count = 0; while (scanner.hasNextInt()) count += scanner.nextInt(); scanner.close(); return count; }
buggyStarterCode.python: def countElements(self, s): iss = iter(s.split()); count = 0; count += int(next(iss)); return count
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function countElements(s) { var scanner = s.split(' '); var count = 0; for (var i = 0; i < scanner.length; i++) count += scanner[i]; return count; }
